root_cause: Repeated liveness probe failures escalated into a kubelet restart loop
evidence: Timeline shows liveness probe failure -> restart -> liveness probe failure -> restart for container 'api'
Repeated probe/restart loop completed within 3.9 minutes with 4 probe-failure occurrence(s) and 2 restart signal(s)
Container 'api' now has restartCount=4, ready=False, state=running
Escalation is based on ordered kubelet probe and restart episodes, not a single failed probe event

causes.message: Container 'api' is repeatedly failing its liveness probe and kubelet is reacting with restarts
The liveness probe failure pattern has escalated into a repeat restart loop rather than a one-off health check miss
Repeated kubelet probe restarts are degrading workload stability

pod:orders-api-7cc9dbf6f8-rx4qw: Liveness probe failures repeatedly triggered kubelet restarts in the same incident window
container:api: Container restartCount=4 after repeated liveness probe restart cycles
Liveness probe failed: HTTP probe failed with statuscode: 500
Container api failed liveness probe, will be restarted

likely_causes: The liveness probe target does not reflect the application's real healthy state
The application briefly recovers after restart but re-enters the same unhealthy condition
Dependencies, warm-up time, or runtime load are causing the same probe-driven restart cycle to repeat
suggested_checks: Review the liveness probe path, command, thresholds, and timing against real application behavior
Correlate restart timestamps with application logs and dependency readiness to explain why the same probe failure keeps recurring
